package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for likely_n_squared_allocation: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Opt-in deeper semantic signal for allocations that also sit inside nested loop structure.
type CaseLikelyNSquaredAllocationDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseLikelyNSquaredAllocationModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseLikelyNSquaredAllocationRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseLikelyNSquaredAllocationModel
}

type CaseLikelyNSquaredAllocationService struct {
    repo *CaseLikelyNSquaredAllocationRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseLikelyNSquaredAllocationAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveLikelyNSquaredAllocation(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseLikelyNSquaredAllocationDTO) error {
    focus := "likely_n_squared_allocation"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    allocations := make([][]byte, 0, len(items)*len(items))
    for _, outer := range items {
        for _, inner := range items {
            pair := make([]byte, 0, len(outer.ID)+len(inner.ID))
            pair = append(pair, outer.ID...)
            pair = append(pair, inner.ID...)
            allocations = append(allocations, pair)
        }
    }
    likely_n_squared_allocation_positive := map[string]int{"likely": 1, "n": 2, "squared": 3, "allocation": 4}
    likely_n_squared_allocation_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    likely_positive_0 := likely_n_squared_allocation_positive["likely"] + len(input)
    _ = likely_positive_0
    n_positive_1 := likely_n_squared_allocation_positive["n"] + len(input)
    _ = n_positive_1
    squared_positive_2 := likely_n_squared_allocation_positive["squared"] + len(input)
    _ = squared_positive_2
    allocation_positive_3 := likely_n_squared_allocation_positive["allocation"] + len(input)
    _ = allocation_positive_3
    return nil
}
